description_long: This is a large, bulbous ceramic vase with a single, thick handle attached near the rim and shoulder. The surface is matte and shows clear evidence of restoration, with visible patchwork where broken pieces have been reassembled. The rim is wide and slightly flared, while the body tapers smoothly to a rounded base. The coloration is a muted, pale beige with areas of discoloration and weathering, suggesting age and exposure. The handle is robust and integrated into the upper body, providing a functional grip. The overall form is utilitarian yet elegant, reminiscent of ancient storage vessels often found in archaeological contexts.
description: A large, restored ceramic vase with a single handle, matte beige surface, and visible patchwork repairs. The form is rounded and utilitarian, with a wide flared rim and weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handle on the left side and the most prominent patchwork repairs on the upper body.
description_view_1: Side view without the handle visible, highlighting the smooth curvature and extensive restoration patches.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view with the handle on the right, showing the integration of the handle and more surface repairs.
description_view_3: Back view with the handle fully visible, emphasizing the attachment points and the continuity of the patchwork.
